app.name: ToraSEO
app.tagline: See the top. Rank the top.
app.altMascotFocused: ToraSEO mascot focused on the analysis

bridgeClosedNotice.title: {{appName}} is closed
bridgeClosedNotice.body: Start {{appName}} again or return home and choose API + AI Chat.
bridgeClosedNotice.restoredBody: You can now close this notice and continue working.

laboratory.sidebarBody: Public-facing quality, formula, and analytics references will live here. Internal eval runs stay outside the public app.

qualityLab.title: Quality Lab
qualityLab.body: This page will show the public part of analysis quality checks: completed checks, comparison progress, confidence notes, and user-safe improvement history.

formulas.title: Formulas
formulas.body: Formula documentation is being prepared. This page will explain public scoring principles without exposing private test cases or internal calibration rules.

toolCatalog.title: Analytics tools list
toolCatalog.sidebarBody: This reference shows which checks belong to each analysis type. It is read-only for now.
toolCatalog.body: This page shows user-facing tool names, their role in analysis, and version changes. Internal keys stay hidden from the interface.
toolCatalog.currentHint: Current table for the active development branch.
toolCatalog.archiveHint: Archive table showing tool state in the selected version.

goalDescriptions.standard: No goal was provided: the report shows both texts, key gaps, similarity risk, and an improvement plan.
goalDescriptions.focusA: The report focuses on Text A: the second text is used as comparison context and a source of reference points.
goalDescriptions.focusB: The report focuses on Text B: the second text is used as comparison context and a source of reference points.
goalDescriptions.beatCompetitor: The report looks for competitor text advantages, gaps in your text, and a plan to strengthen it without copying.
goalDescriptions.styleMatch: The report emphasizes tone, rhythm, clarity, example density, and transferable style techniques without copying phrases.
goalDescriptions.similarity: The report prioritizes exact overlap, semantic closeness, and copying risk.
goalDescriptions.version: The report shows what became stronger or weaker between two text versions.
goalDescriptions.abPost: The report evaluates hook, clarity, brevity, platform fit, and reaction potential.

compare.similarityVerdict: Main finding: similarity and copying risk
compare.styleVerdict: Main finding: style and delivery differences

about.title: About ToraSEO
about.description: ToraSEO is a desktop SEO analysis workspace with MCP + Instructions, API + AI Chat, and structured reports.
about.author: Author:
about.tagline: SEE THE TOP. RANK THE TOP.

settings.title: Settings
settings.comingSoon: Coming soon - settings will live here:

comingSoonItems.configPath: Manual path to Claude Desktop config.json

language.title: Language settings
language.label: Interface language
language.hint: The change is applied after you click Save. Dialogs from the OS (e.g. file picker) keep using your system language.

general.title: General
general.intro: Basic app behavior and quick actions.
general.returnHomeShortcuts: Use keyboard and mouse Back buttons
general.returnHomeShortcutsHint: Off by default. When enabled, the mouse Back button, BrowserBack, and Alt+Left return from settings to the saved analysis session, or from an analysis to the home screen.

unsaved.title: You have unsaved changes
unsaved.body: If you go back now, your changes will be lost.

providers.title: AI providers
providers.intro: Connect an AI provider so the in-app chat can analyze and discuss your scans. OpenRouter is an international model router. RouterAI is a Russian OpenAI-compatible router with ruble billing.
providers.encryptionUnavailable: OS-level secure storage is unavailable on this machine. Configure a system keyring (e.g. gnome-keyring on Linux) before adding API keys.

hints.apiKey: Use the API key from the selected provider account.
hints.apiKeySaved: Saved keys are shown as a mask. ToraSEO cannot reveal the stored secret back into this field.
hints.baseUrl: Leave empty to use the default endpoint: {{url}}
hints.modelId: Paste the exact model ID shown by the provider.

openrouter.name: OpenRouter
openrouter.blurb: International model router. One key can route requests to many global model providers.

routerai.name: RouterAI
routerai.blurb: Russian OpenAI-compatible model router. Use it when ruble billing or Russian payment flow is important.

models.title: {{provider}} models
models.body: Use one provider key, then save the models you want to use inside ToraSEO.
models.empty: Add at least one model for this provider before API + AI Chat can start.
models.modelIdHelp: Use the model ID from the provider model page, not the page link. Example: {{example}}
models.modelIdUrlError: Paste the model ID, not the model page URL. Example: {{example}}
models.modelIdKeyError: Paste the model ID here, not your API key. Example: {{example}}
models.testHelp: Testing is optional and runs a small request against the exact model you choose. It can spend provider tokens on paid models.
models.testOkStructured: Model responds with structured audit output.
models.testOkPlain: Model responds, but structured audit output was not confirmed.

errors.api_key_url: Paste the provider API key here, not a model page URL.
errors.api_key_format: OpenRouter API keys usually start with sk-or-. Paste the key from OpenRouter -> Keys, not a model ID.
errors.encryption_unavailable: Cannot encrypt: OS keyring is not available.
errors.invalid_input: Please fill in the API key.
errors.provider_not_configured: Save the provider key before saving models.
errors.write_failed: Could not save the key. Try again.

modeSelection.question: What would you like to check?
modeSelection.versionFootnote: App version: ToraSEO {{appVersion}} - specific analysis versions are shown in reports and documentation.
modeSelection.claudeOptional: Connection to Claude Desktop is optional

execution.title: Execution mode
execution.confirmFirstBody: Analysis type will become available after you choose the runtime path and confirm it.

bridge.readyTitle: Your Claude Desktop is ready for analysis
bridge.readyBody: ToraSEO can hand Claude Desktop a check or analysis prompt and receive MCP results back into the app.
bridge.codexTitle: Your Codex is ready for analysis
bridge.codexBody: ToraSEO can hand Codex a check or analysis prompt and receive MCP results back into the app.
bridge.codexOnboardingTitle: ToraSEO works in tandem with Codex
bridge.codexOnboardingSubtitle: Three components are required to run. When all three are green, scanning unlocks automatically.
bridge.codexSetupStepOne: Keep ToraSEO open on this screen and make sure Codex is open.
bridge.codexSetupStepTwo: Install the `toraseo-codex-workflow` package into your Codex local skills folder (`~/.codex/skills` by default) so Codex can load it by name.
bridge.codexSetupStepThree: Use the button below, paste the prepared text into Codex, and wait for Codex to confirm that MCP and Codex Workflow Instructions are active.
bridge.codexWorkflowDownloaded: ZIP downloaded ({{tag}}). The folder with the file is open — unzip it into your Codex skills folder so the final path is ~/.codex/skills/toraseo-codex-workflow/SKILL.md. After installing, return here, copy the setup prompt, and run it in Codex.
bridge.codexSetupHint: This setup check does not start a site scan. It only proves that ToraSEO MCP and Codex Workflow Instructions are really active in the current Codex session.
bridge.codexPromptHelperBody: Switch to Codex chat, paste the prompt, and press Enter. If Codex asks for ToraSEO MCP permission, tick the chat/session approval checkbox and click Allow.
bridge.codexChecklistBody: During setup check or analysis, Codex must call verify_codex_workflow_loaded. That proves both ToraSEO MCP access and Codex Workflow Instructions for this session.
bridge.codexMcpLabel: ToraSEO MCP is available to Codex
bridge.codexMcpSetupVerified: Verified in a live Codex setup check. The analysis handshake will verify it again for the active session.
bridge.codexMcpHint: Verified when Codex calls verify_codex_workflow_loaded from the ToraSEO MCP server.
bridge.codexMcpWaiting: Handshake is in progress. ToraSEO is waiting for Codex to call verify_codex_workflow_loaded.
bridge.codexMcpFailed: The last Codex handshake did not verify MCP access for this session.
bridge.codexMcpPending: Not confirmed yet. Run the Codex setup prompt so ToraSEO can verify MCP access.
bridge.codexMcpInstalled: ToraSEO MCP entry was added to {{configPath}}. Restart Codex so it reloads MCP servers.
bridge.codexMcpInstallFailed: Could not update Codex MCP config.
bridge.codexInstructionsSetupVerified: Verified in a live Codex setup check. ToraSEO saw the workflow token from the active Codex session.
bridge.codexInstructionsHint: The handshake token lives only in the Codex Workflow Instructions package and the MCP server.
bridge.codexInstructionsWaiting: Handshake is in progress. ToraSEO is waiting for proof that Codex Workflow Instructions are active.
bridge.codexInstructionsFailed: The last Codex handshake did not verify Codex Workflow Instructions for this session.
bridge.codexInstructionsPending: Not confirmed yet. Run the Codex setup prompt so ToraSEO can verify Codex Workflow Instructions.
bridge.appPathMissing: ToraSEO could not find the path to {{appName}}.
bridge.setupPromptNoticeBody: Open a new session in {{appName}}, paste the prompt, and press Enter. This checks whether {{appName}} can see the ToraSEO SKILL and MCP.

native.providerMissing: Add an AI provider before starting this mode.
native.modelMissing: Add at least one AI provider model in Settings.
native.title: AI provider and model

analysisExit.title: Analysis is still running
analysisExit.body: If you leave now, the unfinished analysis will be stopped and the next launch will start from scratch.

mode.bridgeHint: Run through Claude Desktop and stream MCP facts back into the app.
mode.nativeHint: Run the scan locally, then interpret it with the in-app AI runtime.

siteGroups.basicDescription: URL, indexability, robots.txt, sitemap, and redirects
siteGroups.onPageDescription: Meta tags, canonical, headings, content, and links
siteGroups.advancedDescription: Optional checks that are not needed for every audit

plannedAnalysis.policyBody: The standard score remains 0-100%. The dynamic formula changes the evaluation criteria according to selected tools, but it does not pretend that a larger formula automatically means a cleaner or higher score.
plannedAnalysis.executionBody: This screen is selectable in 0.0.9 so the workflow shape is visible. The analysis run button stays locked until the dedicated tools, prompts, and scoring contract are connected.

sidebar.body: Input and analysis boundaries are prepared here. Full execution will connect after the formula and tool contract is wired.
sidebar.customPlatformHint: Use this when the resource is not in the list; AI will account for that platform's rules.
sidebar.analysisRolePlaceholder: For example: SEO editor, fact-checker, medical editor
sidebar.analysisRoleHint: Leave empty to let AI use the default ToraSEO analysis settings.

platformDetails.site_article: Fits a long-form article with a title, sections, and search intent.
platformDetails.markdown_article: The text looks like a Markdown article; preserve heading structure.
platformDetails.short_social_post: Looks like a short post: a title is usually unnecessary, while clarity and fast meaning matter.
platformDetails.short_article_or_long_social_post: Sits between article and long-post formats; packaging depends on the publishing platform.
platformDetails.default: The format is inferred from the current text and selected tools.

results.title: Analysis results
results.body: Current structured text-analysis results are shown here. The chat answer may be shorter, but the app keeps the checks below.
results.uniquenessHint: Local repetition and template-risk estimate. This is not an internet plagiarism check.
results.syntaxHint: Shows punctuation, sentence-boundary, and overloaded-phrase risk.
results.aiProbabilityHint: Uses AI-style signals: rhythm, templates, and mechanical repetition. This is not proof of authorship.
results.logicHint: Shows text coherence, contradictions, conclusion jumps, and weak cause-and-effect transitions.
results.naturalnessHint: Shows mechanical phrasing, repetition, and uniform-rhythm risk.
results.copySourceFailed: Could not copy the source text.
results.waitingAiReportBody: In API mode, the selected model forms the structured report. ToraSEO will show it here after the AI Chat response.
results.coverageTools: tools completed. Coverage is not a quality score.
results.titleMissingNote: No title was found in the text. A future AI layer can suggest one beside the heading.
results.noDetails: The check completed without extra notes.
results.reportExpectedImpact: Improve clarity, trust, and SEO content quality.
results.reportValidationMethod: Run ToraSEO text analysis again after edits.
results.reportSummary: Structured report for the ToraSEO article text analysis.
results.reportNextStep: Review recommendations, choose priority edits, and scan the text again.
results.strengthsEmpty: Strengths will appear after checks finish.
results.weaknessesEmpty: No clear weaknesses were found by the current tools.
results.hiddenAiReportItems: {{count}} more items are available in the detailed report; only key priorities are shown here.
results.warningDetailsFallback: Warnings found: {{count}}. Review safety and expert-check items before publishing.
results.warningAiLimitation: This is a risk flag, not an expert conclusion: AI can be wrong, so legal, medical, investment, scientific, technical, and calculation-heavy claims still need human verification.
results.strengthMetricDetail: This metric looks strong across the current analysis tools.

verdict.readyDetail: The available tool evidence does not show blocking problems. Final human review is still recommended.
verdict.needsRevisionDetail: The article can improve materially if the highlighted issues are fixed before publication.
verdict.highRiskDetail: One or more core dimensions need attention. Check warnings and priority items before publishing.
verdict.lowCoverageDetail: The analysis has useful signals, but evidence coverage is partial. Treat the result as a guided editing pass.

dimensions.safetyDetail: Risks around prohibited content, rule evasion, legal, medical, investment, technical, scientific, calculation, and external-source review.
dimensions.safetyFix: Review warnings before publishing; AI does not replace a lawyer, doctor, investment adviser, engineer, scientific expert, or manual source check.
dimensions.safetyKeep: No blocking safety or expert-review warnings were found.
dimensions.intentDetail: How clearly the text explains why to read it, what intent it satisfies, and how strong the opening presentation is.
dimensions.intentKeep: Keep the current intent and use the SEO package as a CMS draft.
dimensions.intentFix: Strengthen the first line, reader benefit, and SEO title before publishing.
dimensions.originalityDetail: Local repetition, template risk, naturalness, and AI-style signals.
dimensions.originalityFix: Rewrite repeated fragments with fresher examples and less uniform phrasing.
dimensions.originalityKeep: Keep the specific examples and avoid adding generic filler in later edits.
dimensions.clarityDetail: Syntax, sentence density, structure, headings, and scanability.
dimensions.clarityFix: Shorten dense sentences and add clearer section breaks before polishing style.
dimensions.clarityKeep: Use the current structure as the base and polish only the weak sections.
dimensions.logicDetail: Internal contradictions, weak transitions, and unsupported conclusions.
dimensions.logicFix: Add missing intermediate reasoning where the text jumps from claim to conclusion.
dimensions.logicKeep: Preserve the current argument chain and verify new claims after editing.
dimensions.trustDetail: Fact-sensitive claims, vague authorities, exact numbers, and hallucination risk.
dimensions.trustFix: Verify exact numbers and soften claims that are not supported by concrete evidence.
dimensions.trustOptional: Run optional trust checks for claim-heavy medical, legal, finance, or technical articles.
dimensions.platformDetail: Publishing context, tone, audience, language, and media placement.
dimensions.platformFix: Add media markers only where they clarify the article, not as decoration.
dimensions.platformKeep: Keep platform-specific packaging separate from the article body.

intentForecast.title: Intent forecast and SEO package
intentForecast.noInternet: This is a local forecast without SERP or social-platform data. Internet verification can be connected later through a separate external source.
intentForecast.hookTooltip: The hook shows how strongly the opening lines catch the reader: whether the pain, benefit, or promised result is visible.
intentForecast.ctrTooltip: CTR is a local estimate of title and description click potential. It is not real search-result statistics.
intentForecast.trendTooltip: Trend is an approximate local estimate of topic potential from the text wording. Internet demand is not checked here.

results.nextActionRewrite: Ask AI for a bounded rewrite
results.priorityDefaultDetail: No blocking findings were detected. Keep edits focused and re-run analysis after the final draft.

descriptions.platform: Detects the publishing platform and context.
descriptions.structure: Checks headings, paragraphs, and article structure.
descriptions.style: Detects text style and weak spots.
descriptions.tone: Reviews whether the tone fits the topic and audience.
descriptions.audience: Checks language, audience, and explanation level.
descriptions.media: Checks where and how media markers are placed.
descriptions.uniqueness: Estimates local repetition and template risk.
descriptions.syntax: Checks syntax and clear language risks.
descriptions.ai: Estimates AI-style signals in the text.
descriptions.aiTrace: Maps local AI-like fragments without claiming authorship proof.
descriptions.genericness: Checks whether the text is too broad, watery, or template-like.
descriptions.readabilityComplexity: Checks sentence density and heavy paragraphs.
descriptions.claimQueue: Queues claims that need source verification.
descriptions.naturalness: Looks for mechanical phrasing and repetition.
descriptions.facts: Looks for disputed facts and overconfident claims.
descriptions.hallucination: Looks for invented details and AI hallucination signals.
descriptions.intent: Evaluates intent, opening pitch, and SEO package.
descriptions.safety: Finds legal, medical, scientific, and technical risks.

issueMessages.thin_text: The text is short for a search-oriented article; expand the useful answer before optimizing.
issueMessages.low_paragraph_structure: The text has too few clear paragraphs, which makes scanning harder.
issueMessages.weak_heading_structure: Add clear sections or subheadings so structure is easier to read.
issueMessages.long_sentences: Average sentence length is high; split dense phrases.
issueMessages.formal_phrasing: The text contains formal or mechanical phrasing; make key explanations more direct.
issueMessages.tone_review: Keep tone aligned with topic risk: caveats should help the reader without weighing down every paragraph.
issueMessages.audience_fit: Check that examples, terminology, and explanation level fit the intended reader.
issueMessages.media_markers_present: Media markers are already present in the body; keep them near the relevant explanation.
issueMessages.no_media_markers: No media markers were found. Add them only where image, video, animation, or audio improves understanding.
issueMessages.uniqueness_risk: The text has local repetition or duplicate-pattern risk. This is not an internet plagiarism check.
issueMessages.duplicate_sentences: Some sentences repeat almost exactly inside the article.
issueMessages.syntax_risk: The text has visible syntax or punctuation risks that should be checked before publishing.
issueMessages.dense_sentences: Several sentences are dense; grammar may be correct, but readability suffers.
issueMessages.ai_style_probability: The text has AI-style signals: generic transitions, uniform rhythm, or repeated words.
issueMessages.ai_trace_map_risk: Several fragments look AI-assisted: generic transitions, formal wording, repetition, or overly even rhythm.
issueMessages.generic_watery_text: The text may feel watery: it uses broad statements or service phrases instead of concrete evidence.
issueMessages.low_concrete_evidence: The article has few examples, numbers, sources, cases, or practical details for its length.
issueMessages.readability_complexity_risk: Dense sentences or heavy paragraphs make the text harder to read than it needs to be.
issueMessages.heavy_paragraphs: One or more paragraphs are heavy enough to slow scanning.
issueMessages.claim_source_queue: Some claims should enter an editor's source-check queue before publication.
issueMessages.fact_distortion_risk: The text contains fact-sensitive claims that may need source verification before publication.
issueMessages.absolute_claims: Absolute wording can distort meaning when the text does not prove the claim.
issueMessages.sensitive_claims_without_sources: Medical, legal, financial, or technical claims should be supported by sources or cautious wording.
issueMessages.possible_internal_contradiction: The text may contain statements that pull conclusions in different directions.
issueMessages.unsupported_causality: Some cause-and-effect transitions may need examples, data, or intermediate reasoning.
issueMessages.hallucination_risk: Factual details created or processed by AI may need verification.
issueMessages.vague_authorities: Phrases like "experts say" or "studies show" should point to a concrete source.
issueMessages.low_ctr_potential: The title or opening does not yet create enough click or reading motivation.
issueMessages.weak_hook: The opening hook can be stronger: show the reader problem, conflict, or clear benefit earlier.
issueMessages.unsafe_or_evasion_intent: The text may contain unsafe or rule-evasion intent and needs manual review.
issueMessages.legal_review_needed: The text contains legally sensitive claims. It should not be presented as legal advice without review.
issueMessages.medical_review_needed: The text contains medical or health-sensitive claims. It should not replace clinician review or source verification.
issueMessages.investment_review_needed: The text contains investment-sensitive claims. It should not be presented as personal investment advice.
issueMessages.technical_engineering_review_needed: The text contains technical or engineering claims that may need expert verification, drawings, standards, or manufacturer documentation.
issueMessages.scientific_review_needed: The text contains research or scientific-method claims that may need methodology, sources, or calculation review.
issueMessages.calculation_review_needed: The text contains several numeric or formula-like fragments; calculations may need a dedicated check.
issueMessages.custom_resource_rules_needed: A custom platform or resource may require manual rule verification.
issueMessages.external_verification_needed: External source, jurisdiction, platform, SERP, or analytics verification was not performed by this local text scan.
issueMessages.repeated_termsPrefix: Repeated terms may make the text feel mechanical

recommendations.platform: Keep platform metadata separate from the body: title, description, tags, and preview text.
recommendations.structure: Use one clear main title and group sections by intent.
recommendations.style: Add concrete verbs, shorter sentences, and examples where the reader may hesitate.
recommendations.tone: Use caveats precisely: they should protect the reader without making the whole text defensive.
recommendations.audience: Name the target reader in the introduction when the topic can serve several audiences.
recommendations.media: Place media markers inside relevant sections, not at the end of the text.
recommendations.uniqueness: Rewrite repeated fragments with new examples, narrower claims, or clearer transitions.
recommendations.syntax: Run a final human pass for punctuation, sentence boundaries, and overloaded phrases.
recommendations.ai: To sound more authorial, add specific examples, context, and more varied sentence rhythm.
recommendations.aiTrace: Use the trace map as an editing guide: replace generic transitions with examples, sources, or sharper author judgment.
recommendations.genericness: Turn broad paragraphs into useful ones by adding a concrete example, source, number, or reader action.
recommendations.readabilityComplexity: Shorten dense sentences and split heavy paragraphs before polishing style.
recommendations.claimQueue: Verify queued claims against sources; if a source is weak, soften or remove the claim.
recommendations.naturalness: Vary sentence openings and remove service phrases that do not add meaning.
recommendations.logic: Check places with “therefore”, “because”, “always”, and “never”; they need enough support.
recommendations.facts: Verify numbers, names, and sensitive claims; soften statements that cannot be supported confidently.
recommendations.hallucination: Replace vague authorities with concrete sources or remove details that cannot be verified.
recommendations.intentSeo: Strengthen the first line, clear reader benefit, and SEO package before publishing.
recommendations.safetyScience: Check legal, medical, investment, technical, scientific, and country-sensitive areas with experts, platform rules, or official sources.

results.pageMentionsTitle: Page mentions in search and external resources
results.pageMentionsEmpty: The link list is empty because public search mentions require a connected search index, SERP API, or owner data.

toraRank.previewNote: Early app score: fewer tools lowers the score ceiling.
toraRank.modalBody: Tora Rank shows the total strength of this text-analysis result. It is not a search position and not a traffic guarantee: MCP tools provide metrics, and the app combines them into an early cgs score.
toraRank.chatBoundary: The AI chat does not calculate Tora Rank as a separate tool. If you ask about the score, it should explain it as an app preview layer above the analysis results.
toraRank.raiseStructure: clear structure and a logical reader path
toraRank.raiseIntent: clear intent, strong hook, and SEO-ready packaging
toraRank.lowerRepetition: watery text, repetition, and template phrases
toraRank.lowerLogic: weak logic, contradictions, and unclear conclusions
toraRank.exampleFormula: text strength + intent + usefulness - penalties = final Tora Rank in cgs

forms.intentPlaceholder: What should this page rank for or explain?
forms.optionalTextBlockPlaceholder: Paste a specific fragment if only part of the page should be analyzed.
forms.compareGoalPlaceholder: For example: find weaknesses in article B, improve my text, or compare both versions neutrally.
forms.aiDraftPromptCopied: Prompt copied. Paste it into {{appName}} chat so AI can provide a solution.
forms.scanPromptCopied: Prompt copied. Paste it into {{appName}} chat so AI can continue this analysis.
forms.aiDraftSentToApiChat: Request sent to API + AI Chat so AI can provide a solution.
forms.scanSentToApiChat: Report formed, and the text was sent to API + AI Chat for a short explanation.
forms.aiChatOpenFailed: Could not open API + AI Chat. Check the provider and try again.
forms.bridgeRunFailed: Could not start analysis. Check MCP + Instructions mode and try again.
forms.compareNeedTexts: Add both texts before starting comparison.
forms.compareSentToApiChat: Comparison started in API + AI Chat. The report will appear here after the model responds.
forms.compareBridgeUnavailable: Two-text comparison started through MCP + Instructions. Results will appear here after the tools finish.
forms.compareSkillFallbackPromptCopied: Prompt copied. Paste it into {{appName}} chat: the Skill will compare the two texts directly in chat while MCP or the app is unavailable.
forms.siteCompareBridgeOnly: Site comparison by URL is available in API + AI Chat and MCP + Instructions. Choose a mode on the home screen.
forms.siteCompareNeedUrls: Add at least two URLs for comparison.
forms.siteCompareStarted: Comparison started through MCP + Instructions. The result will appear below as one comparative dashboard.
forms.siteCompareApiStarted: AI Chat opened. ToraSEO is scanning the sites, then AI will form the comparison report.
forms.aiDraftNeedContext: Add at least a topic, query, or short text draft so AI can suggest a useful solution.
forms.aiDraftNeedMore: Write a bit more: at least two short paragraphs, so AI has enough context for a useful solution.
forms.aiDraftPrepared: This looks like enough context for a solution. Full execution will connect with text analysis.
forms.targetQueryPlaceholder: Main keyword, topic, or user intent
forms.textTopicPlaceholder: Topic, title, or intent. If the body has its own title, the body title wins.
forms.designFocusPlaceholder: Conversion, readability, page trust, visual hierarchy, or content UX

pageByUrl.title: Page source
pageByUrl.body: ToraSEO will extract the main article text from HTML, skip ads and service blocks, then run checks like text analysis.

articleCompare.title: Two article versions
articleCompare.body: Compare two texts by intent, structure, completeness, style, trust, similarity risk, and improvement plan.

siteCompare.title: Comparable sites
siteCompare.body: Compare up to three sites through one dashboard: who is stronger, why, where the gap is, and what to fix first.

imageAnalysis.title: Image analysis
imageAnalysis.body: Image upload, OCR, visual content checks, and media SEO recommendations are in development.

forms.pageByUrlNeedUrl: Enter the full page URL: https://...
forms.pageByUrlSentToApiChat: Page by URL analysis started in API + AI Chat. The report will appear here after the model responds.
forms.pageByUrlSkillFallbackPromptCopied: Prompt copied. Paste it into {{appName}} so AI can analyze the page through Skill while MCP or the app is unavailable.
forms.pageByUrlPromptCopied: Page by URL analysis started through MCP + Instructions. Results will appear here after the tools finish.
forms.bridgeUnavailable: {{appName}} is unavailable. Open it before starting bridge analysis.
forms.siteCompareFocusPlaceholder: Example: compare my site with competitors, find metadata and content gaps, and understand what to borrow from the leader.
forms.scanNeedText: Add article text to prepare the analysis prompt.

compare.bridgeSummary: Structured comparison of two texts based on ToraSEO MCP tool data.
compare.bridgeNextStep: Review gaps, similarity risk, and the improvement plan, then strengthen the target text without copying the other one.
compare.weakStructureDetail: Fewer visible sections, lists, or support blocks. Check the reader path from problem to solution.
compare.weakReadabilityDetail: Average sentence length is higher. This is not always an error, but long sentences should be checked for overload.
compare.weakSpecificityDetail: Fewer numbers, lists, steps, or practical signals. Add them only where they are accurate and useful.

goalDescriptions.standard: No goal is set: the report shows both texts, key gaps, similarity risk, and an improvement plan.
goalDescriptions.focusA: The report is focused on text A: the second text is used as comparison context and a source of reference points.
goalDescriptions.focusB: The report is focused on text B: the second text is used as comparison context and a source of reference points.
goalDescriptions.beatCompetitor: The report looks for competitor text advantages, gaps in your text, and a plan to strengthen the material without copying.
goalDescriptions.styleMatch: The report focuses on tone, rhythm, clarity, example density, and transferable style techniques without copying phrases.
goalDescriptions.similarity: The report prioritizes exact overlap, semantic closeness, and copying risk.
goalDescriptions.version: The report shows what became stronger or weaker between two text versions.
goalDescriptions.abPost: The report evaluates hook, clarity, brevity, platform fit, and reaction potential.

compare.similarityVerdict: Main finding: similarity and borrowing risk
compare.styleVerdict: Main finding: style and presentation differences
compare.versionVerdict: Main finding: what changed between versions
compare.competitorVerdict: Main finding: text gaps against competitor
compare.winnerA: Text A is stronger by text signals
compare.winnerB: Text B is stronger by text signals
compare.winnerTie: The texts are close: category gaps matter more
compare.defaultGap: The main gap may be intent, completeness, structure, specificity, or trust. Check the categories below before editing.
compare.platformDetail: The result compares only text: no domain, links, technical SEO, or live SERP.

metrics.structureDetail: Uses headings and lists as local structure signals. Structure quality matters more than count alone.
metrics.specificityDetail: Local signal for numbers, examples, and steps. Specificity helps only when wording is accurate.
metrics.readabilityDetail: Shorter sentences are usually easier to scan, but expert text sometimes needs longer explanations.
metrics.similarityDetail: Local check of matching phrases in two texts. This is not an external plagiarism database.

compare.copyRiskHigh: Exact overlap is high. Use the stronger text as a reference, but independently rewrite structure, examples, and wording.
compare.copyRiskMedium: Some exact overlap exists. Keep useful ideas, but add your own examples and do not repeat phrasing.
compare.copyRiskLow: Local exact phrase overlap is low. This does not guarantee uniqueness: semantic similarity still needs to be evaluated by the findings below.
compare.limitTextOnly: This is text-only comparison: domain age, links, technical SEO, speed, and behavior signals are not included.
compare.limitSimilarity: Similarity risk is a local heuristic, not an internet plagiarism check.
compare.limitFacts: Fact-sensitive, legal, medical, financial, and technical claims still require sources or expert review.
compare.waitingInputBody: Add text A and text B, then run comparison. The report will be shown in two columns.
compare.waitingReportBody: The selected mode compares both texts. ToraSEO will show the result here after structured data appears.
compare.title: Comparison result
compare.gapsDescription: Which topics, semantic blocks, and useful elements differ between texts A and B.
compare.visualGraphBody: Graphs show relative local signals. This is not a final SEO score, but a quick way to see gaps.
compare.sideFindingsBody: If no analysis goal is set, ToraSEO shows a standard comparative report for both texts.
compare.focusedFindingsBody: Because the goal clearly points to one text, the block below primarily shows findings for that text.
compare.noStrengths: Clear strengths will appear after checks finish.
compare.noWeaknesses: No clear weaknesses were found by the current local signals.
compare.actionPlanDescription: Editing priorities: strengthen the target text without copying the second one.

hookTypes.problemSolution: SEO hook for title and intro

jurisdiction.ruLanguageAssumed: Russian language: applicable country needs review
jurisdiction.ruLanguageInternationalPlatform: Russian text for an international platform: platform and publication-country rules are needed

extract_main_text.label: Extract main text
extract_main_text.tooltip: Find the main article, post, or content block on the page

analyze_google_page_search.label: Google page check
analyze_google_page_search.tooltip: Check what can be known about the page in Google; clicks and impressions require Search Console or an official SEO provider

analyze_yandex_page_search.label: Yandex page check
analyze_yandex_page_search.tooltip: Check what can be known about the page in Yandex; clicks and impressions require Yandex Webmaster/Metrica or an official SEO provider

detect_text_platform.label: Platform detection
detect_text_platform.tooltip: Adapt the analysis to site articles, X, Facebook, LinkedIn, Habr, Reddit, or a custom resource

analyze_text_structure.label: Text structure
analyze_text_structure.tooltip: Evaluate title, opening, sections, transitions, and completeness

analyze_text_style.label: Text style
analyze_text_style.tooltip: Check whether the text style matches the selected goal and platform

analyze_tone_fit.label: Tone fit
analyze_tone_fit.tooltip: Check whether the text sounds businesslike, educational, humorous, personal, or another chosen style

language_audience_fit.label: Language and audience
language_audience_fit.tooltip: Warn when the text language does not fit the selected platform audience

media_placeholder_review.label: Media placement
media_placeholder_review.tooltip: Use image, video, animation, and audio placeholders in the text when giving recommendations

naturalness_indicators.label: Naturalness
naturalness_indicators.tooltip: Look for robotic phrasing, repetition, and weak human readability signals

intent_seo_forecast.label: Intent and promotion forecast
intent_seo_forecast.tooltip: Estimate intent, hook, CTR and trend potential, and prepare a CMS SEO package

safety_science_review.label: Risk and expert review
safety_science_review.tooltip: Flag unsafe, legal, medical, scientific, technical, calculation, and other expert-review risks

article_uniqueness.label: Article uniqueness
article_uniqueness.tooltip: Estimate local repetition and template risk without claiming internet plagiarism detection

language_syntax.label: Language syntax
language_syntax.tooltip: Check grammar-adjacent syntax, punctuation, and sentence boundary risks

ai_writing_probability.label: AI writing probability
ai_writing_probability.tooltip: Estimate AI-style signals from rhythm, repetition, and generic phrasing

ai_trace_map.label: AI trace map
ai_trace_map.tooltip: Map AI-like fragments by generic transitions, formal wording, repetition, and uniform rhythm

genericness_water_check.label: Genericness and watery text
genericness_water_check.tooltip: Find broad phrasing, weak specificity, repeated concepts, and places that need concrete evidence

readability_complexity.label: Readability and complexity
readability_complexity.tooltip: Review sentence density, long sentences, and heavy paragraphs

claim_source_queue.label: Claim source queue
claim_source_queue.tooltip: Collect claims, numbers, and absolute wording that should be source-checked before publication

fact_distortion_check.label: Fact distortion
fact_distortion_check.tooltip: Find claims, numbers, and absolute wording that may distort facts without source verification

logic_consistency_check.label: Logic check
logic_consistency_check.tooltip: Check internal contradictions, conclusion jumps, and weak cause-and-effect transitions

ai_hallucination_check.label: AI and hallucination check
ai_hallucination_check.tooltip: Check AI involvement signals, vague authorities, and factual details that may have been invented

compare_article_structure.label: Structure comparison
compare_article_structure.tooltip: Compare how two texts organize the same idea

compare_intent_gap.label: Intent comparison
compare_intent_gap.tooltip: Check whether both texts answer the same user request

compare_content_gap.label: Content Gap
compare_content_gap.tooltip: Find important topics and sections present in one text and missing in the other

compare_semantic_gap.label: Semantic coverage
compare_semantic_gap.tooltip: Compare entities, concepts, and subtopics in both texts

compare_specificity_gap.label: Specificity comparison
compare_specificity_gap.tooltip: Compare examples, numbers, steps, scenarios, and practical usefulness

compare_trust_gap.label: Trust comparison
compare_trust_gap.tooltip: Compare sources, caveats, warnings, and risky claims

compare_article_style.label: Style comparison
compare_article_style.tooltip: Compare tone, clarity, rhythm, and platform fit

similarity_risk.label: Similarity risk
similarity_risk.tooltip: Estimate local exact phrase overlap and copying risk between the two texts

compare_title_ctr.label: Title and click
compare_title_ctr.tooltip: Compare headline clarity, promise, intent fit, and click potential

compare_platform_fit.label: Platform fit comparison
compare_platform_fit.tooltip: Compare which text better fits the selected platform or resource

compare_strengths_weaknesses.label: Strengths and weaknesses
compare_strengths_weaknesses.tooltip: Show strong and weak sides side by side

compare_improvement_plan.label: Improvement plan
compare_improvement_plan.tooltip: Build a plan for strengthening the target text without copying the other text

compare_site_positioning.label: Positioning comparison
compare_site_positioning.tooltip: Compare site promise, audience, and visible offer

compare_site_metadata.label: Metadata comparison
compare_site_metadata.tooltip: Compare title, description, canonical, Open Graph, and Twitter Card coverage

compare_site_structure.label: Structure comparison
compare_site_structure.tooltip: Compare headings, internal links, URL delivery, and page architecture signals

compare_site_content_depth.label: Content depth comparison
compare_site_content_depth.tooltip: Compare topical coverage and visible content completeness

compare_site_technical_basics.label: Technical baseline comparison
compare_site_technical_basics.tooltip: Compare basic SEO and public technical signals

compare_site_delta.label: Delta comparison
compare_site_delta.tooltip: Show where each site wins or loses by direction

compare_site_direction_matrix.label: Direction matrix
compare_site_direction_matrix.tooltip: Show a compact heatmap for robots, sitemap, metadata, canonical, content, redirects, and stack

compare_site_competitive_insights.label: Competitive insights
compare_site_competitive_insights.tooltip: Explain who is stronger, why, what to borrow, and what to fix first

visual_hierarchy.label: Visual hierarchy
visual_hierarchy.tooltip: Planned design-readability signal for future visual analysis

content_ux.label: Content UX
content_ux.tooltip: Check how content supports scanning, trust, and conversion

design_content_alignment.label: Design-content alignment
design_content_alignment.tooltip: Check whether layout and content reinforce the same message

trust_signals.label: Trust signals
trust_signals.tooltip: Review visible credibility markers such as contacts, proof, authorship, and policies

siteAudit.enterUrl: Enter a site URL in the sidebar
siteAudit.confirmCancelScan:
Cancel the current analysis?

Scan results will be lost.

sitemap.label: Sitemap
sitemap.tooltip: Sitemap discovery and indexed pages list

indexability.label: Indexability
indexability.tooltip: Robots.txt and meta robots without live search-index claims

links.label: Links
links.tooltip: Internal, external, and invalid links in main content

stack.label: Stack detection
stack.tooltip: CMS, builder, framework, analytics, CDN, and server signals

onboarding.lockedSidebar:
Finish the setup on the right
to continue
onboarding.title: ToraSEO works in tandem with Claude Desktop
onboarding.subtitle: Three components are required to run. When all three are green, scanning unlocks automatically.

claude.openFailedFallback: Could not open Claude Desktop. Launch it manually.

mcp.label: ToraSEO MCP server is connected
mcp.hintMissing: ToraSEO can add MCP automatically. If your config lives in a non-standard location, point us to it manually.
mcp.installInfo: ToraSEO MCP entry was added to {{configPath}}. Restart Claude Desktop to reload MCP servers.
mcp.installError: Could not update Claude Desktop MCP config.
mcp.errorParse: The file is not valid JSON. Open it in an editor and check the format.
mcp.infoNoToraseoYet: File selected, but the mcpServers.toraseo entry is not there yet. Use automatic MCP configuration to add it.

skill.label: Claude Bridge Instructions are installed
skill.hintMissing: Download the ZIP from GitHub and install it via Claude Desktop: Settings → Skills → Install ZIP. Then click "I installed Claude Bridge Instructions".
skill.hintFilesystem: File found in Claude local skills folder
skill.errorNoRelease: No Claude Bridge Instructions release found on GitHub. Open the releases page manually.
skill.infoDownloaded: ZIP downloaded ({{tag}}). The folder with the file is open — drag it into Claude Desktop: Settings → Skills → Install from ZIP. After installing, return here and click "I installed Claude Bridge Instructions".
skill.confirmDialog:
Confirm that the ToraSEO Claude Bridge Instructions are installed in Claude Desktop via Settings → Skills?

Without those instructions loaded into Claude's context, the analysis will be incomplete — it will ignore CRAWLING_POLICY, verdict mapping, and the CGS scoring formula.
skill.tooltipFilesystem: Claude Bridge Instructions are detected via the filesystem — no confirmation needed
skill.tooltipManual: Manual confirmation already active — reset it below to confirm again

onboarding.appPathMissing: ToraSEO could not find the path to {{appName}}.

preflight.depsFailed: Dependency check failed. Open Claude Desktop, make sure MCP is connected and Claude Bridge Instructions are installed.
preflight.providerMissing: Add an AI provider before using API + AI Chat.
preflight.modelMissing: Choose the AI provider model before starting analysis.
preflight.nativeUnavailable: API + AI Chat is unavailable in this build.
preflight.articleTextBridgeRequired: Text analysis through Codex or Claude Desktop requires MCP + Instructions mode.
preflight.siteCompareBridgeRequired: Site comparison by URL can run through API + AI Chat or MCP + Instructions.
preflight.skillFallbackCopyFailed: Claude Bridge Instructions are installed, but ToraSEO could not copy the fallback prompt.
preflight.codexNeedsConfirmation: Open Codex before starting the Codex bridge path.
preflight.codexSetupMissing: Run the Codex setup check first so ToraSEO can confirm MCP and Codex Workflow Instructions.
preflight.codexClosed: Codex is closed. Open Codex to continue.
preflight.codexClosedDuringScan: Codex closed during the bridge flow. The active Codex scan was cancelled.

downloaded.title: Update ready
downloaded.body: ToraSEO {{version}} downloaded. The app will restart to install.

check.found: Update found: {{version}}. See the notification in the corner.
check.noneFound: No updates found. You're on version {{version}}.
check.alreadyDownloaded: Update {{version}} is already downloaded and ready to install. See the notification in the corner.
check.alreadyDownloading: Update is already downloading. See the notification in the corner.

updater.installerCallout: Important: a fresh install (running the .exe directly) shows a regular installer.

chat.endedBody: Start a new API + AI Chat analysis in the main ToraSEO window to continue.
chat.nativeReady: Native mode is ready. You can ask how the audit works now, then run a scan for site-specific recommendations.
chat.articleTextIntro: API text analysis is open. AI will use the selected checks to form the report.
chat.articleCompareIntro: API text comparison is open. AI will use the selected checks to form the report.
chat.siteCompareIntro: API site comparison is open. ToraSEO will collect URL checks first, then AI will form the comparison report.
chat.bridgeReady: Bridge mode is ready. Paste the copied prompt into Claude Desktop to let MCP tools fill the app.
chat.noScanContext: I can explain the ToraSEO workflow now. For site- or article-specific analysis, start the matching analysis from the main ToraSEO window first.
chat.autoInterpretationPrompt: Interpret the completed site audit and give prioritized recommendations.

helper.bridgeNoScan: Click Scan to copy the Bridge prompt, then send it in Claude Desktop.
helper.bridgeAwaiting: Prompt copied. Claude is expected to call the handshake next.
helper.bridgeRunning: Claude is running MCP tools. Results flow into the right panel automatically.
helper.bridgeComplete: Bridge scan finished. Claude recommendations can continue in the external chat.
helper.nativeNoScan: You can ask how the analysis works now. Run a scan when you want site-specific recommendations.
helper.nativeArticleScan: Article context is ready. The first analysis uses Strict mode.
helper.nativeArticleSolution: Article context is ready. Ideas mode is used for solution drafts.

inputPlaceholder.native: Ask about the current site audit...
inputPlaceholder.bridge: Bridge mode uses Claude Desktop for the live conversation.

siteDashboard.healthNote: This is a score for selected checks, not a traffic, popularity, or ranking metric.
siteDashboard.noSignalsYet: Signals will appear here as tools return structured facts.
siteDashboard.startPrompt: Run the default audit preset to fill this board with site facts, metrics, and next actions.

analysisPanel.title: Analysis Results
analysisPanel.subtitle: Facts, hypotheses, priority, export
analysisPanel.startHint: Enter a site URL, select tools, and click Scan.
analysisPanel.emptyOverview: Run a scan to populate the analysis panel.
analysisPanel.noHypotheses: No hypotheses are available for the current report.
analysisPanel.noReport: No report has been generated yet.
analysisPanel.nextStepFix: Fix first: {{items}}. Run the scan again after edits.
analysisPanel.nextStepClean: No critical issues found. Review informational notes and run the scan again after edits.
